findings.title: Activation reads explicitly batched in one parallel round
findings.detail: On Activation instructs batching config files, memory-system.md, sidecar index (or init.md), and course_context.yaml without false ordering dependencies. Reduces wall-clock latency versus sequential tool rounds.

findings.title: Sidecar index as single memory entry point
findings.detail: Loads ./references/memory-system.md then _bmad/memory/content-creator-sidecar/index.md as the router for what else to load, with init.md only when the sidecar is absent. Matches selective loading guidance and avoids loading patterns/chronology by default.

findings.title: patterns.md and chronology.md deferred until needed
findings.detail: Sidecar docs distinguish load-on-activation (index, access-boundaries per file) from load-when-needed for append-only history files. Reduces token load on typical activations.

findings.title: Capability table routes references per task
findings.detail: Internal capabilities map to specific reference files (e.g. pedagogical-framework vs templates vs delegation-protocol) instead of loading the full reference set every time. IA/LO/BT/CL/CS share one framework file—one read covers multiple related capabilities.

findings.title: No explicit parallel fan-out for multiple independent writer briefs
findings.detail: delegation-protocol.md requires splitting mixed-type work into separate briefs (one per writer). The headless flow does not state that independent writer delegations should be invoked in parallel in one message round when pieces have no dependency on each other. Sequential delegation adds latency when two or three writers could run concurrently.
findings.action: Add one bullet under headless or delegation-protocol: when briefs are independent (no shared draft dependency), issue writer delegations in parallel in a single turn; serialize only when output of one brief feeds another.

findings.title: Writer and editorial handoffs lack strict return-shape constraints
findings.detail: Delegation briefs list required input fields for writers but do not specify succinct return formats, token budgets, or JSON-only structured responses for Paige/Sophia/Caravaggio or editorial reviewers. Verbose returns inflate parent context and slow review.
findings.action: Add a subsection requiring bounded output: e.g. max sections, optional JSON fields for draft body vs metadata, and 'only return the draft and a short alignment note' language for each agent class.

findings.title: Large or many source_materials lack multi-source delegation pattern
findings.detail: Optional context envelope fields include source_materials but the skill does not state that five or more independent sources should be summarized or chunked via delegated reads. A parent could ingest large corpora sequentially and bloat context before delegating prose.
findings.action: When source_materials exceeds a threshold (e.g. 5 files or N tokens), instruct: delegate parallel sub-reads or extraction subagents, pass only excerpts plus citations into writer briefs; parent coordinates and does not load full sources when avoidable.

findings.title: Style bible reads not explicitly batched
findings.detail: Activation batching is explicit for config and memory paths, but 'read style bible fresh from resources/style-bible/' does not repeat the batch-parallel-read guidance. Minor latency/token risk if many files are opened one-by-one.
findings.action: Add one line: batch all needed style-bible file reads in one round when multiple files are required.

findings.title: Editorial prose-then-structure order is dependency-correct
findings.detail: Step 2 runs prose review before structure review on assembled artifacts. That sequencing is appropriate when structure review depends on edited text—not a missed parallelization.

prepass.note: No automated sequential-loop or dependency-graph issues; judgment focused on documented runtime patterns.

execution_efficiency_dimensions.parallelization: Strong activation batching; gap on documenting parallel multi-writer and multi-source extraction fan-out.
execution_efficiency_dimensions.subagent_delegation: Clear brief inputs and writer matrix; output contracts and token discipline under-specified for downstream agents.
execution_efficiency_dimensions.memory_loading: Strong—index routing, deferred patterns/chronology, no caching of style bible in sidecar.
execution_efficiency_dimensions.context_optimization: Role keeps prose in writers; remaining risk is large source_materials and verbose returns without bounds.

summary.assessment: Pre-pass reported no structural dependency issues. The skill scores well on memory discipline and activation batching; the main efficiency gaps are optional parallel writer/source delegation guidance and tighter return-format constraints for delegated agents.
